label: Non-ankle sprain or strain with negative x-ray
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of sprain or strain outside the ankle pathway.
X-ray was performed and documented negative for fracture.
No suspected ligament rupture, tendon rupture, occult fracture pattern, septic joint, compartment syndrome, inability to bear weight for lower-extremity sites, or high-risk site modifier.
exclusion_criteria: Ankle sprain pathway, no x-ray performed, positive fracture, dislocation, open wound, open fracture, high-energy trauma, or crush injury.

Suspected tendon rupture, ligament rupture, scaphoid tenderness pattern, compartment syndrome concern, septic joint or infection concern, neurovascular compromise, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
Inability to bear weight for lower-extremity injuries, pediatric growth plate pathway, or elderly/osteoporotic high-risk knee, shoulder, or wrist injury.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, sports medicine, or orthopedics in 5 to 7 days if pain, swelling, movement, or walking is not improving, or sooner if your clinician gave a specific plan.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain framing, several-week recovery for bad sprains, elevation/protection concepts, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, or inability to move. Ankle-specific sources support general sprain aftercare principles only. ED use requires documented non-ankle site, x-ray performed and negative, intact neurovascular exam, and strong site-specific blockers.

condition_terms: sprain strain non ankle xray negative
high_confidence_terms: sprain strain non ankle xray negative

review.notes: Phase 205 clinician review applied from multi-phenotype packet. Highest-risk draft in this batch; keep draft-only until stress across knee, shoulder, wrist, elbow, and foot passes and clinician owner approves final promotion.
